suggestions.title: Hierarchical annotation v2: scale to >=200 rows with full human review
suggestions.description: Extend the v1 pilot to >=200 rows by re-running the upstream pilot pipeline with a stricter retry policy (eliminate the 11 FrontierScience-Olympiad rows where steps==null), then perform a full human-rater review of every row. Compute inter-rater agreement (Krippendorff's alpha or Cohen's kappa) between the human rater and the LLM annotator.

suggestions.title: Re-run LLM-as-judge with full problem text (no truncation)
suggestions.description: The v1 judge sees only the first 1500 chars of each problem. Three of four needs-revision verdicts on FrontierScience-Olympiad rows complain about content not present in the truncated excerpt. Re-run the audit using the full problem text (or a structured per-section summary) and compare accept rates. Predict an absolute accept-rate increase of >=15 percentage points on FrontierScience-Olympiad.

suggestions.title: Reconcile WorkArena++ flat-action sequences with the three-level schema
suggestions.description: The judge rejected all three WorkArena++ rows because the upstream annotation lacks `conceptual` nodes, causing the mapper to emit empty subtask lists. Investigate whether the WorkArena++ source carries an implicit subtask boundary (e.g., screen transitions) that can be detected automatically, or alternatively change the v2 schema to accept flat atomic-only rows as a distinct hierarchy_kind. Document the chosen approach and update the mapper.

suggestions.title: Remediate proxy benchmark naming and task_id non-uniqueness
suggestions.description: The pilot file uses tau-bench and WorkArena++ as proxies but task_id prefixes are still `he_*` (HumanEval) and `m2w_*` (Mind2Web) from earlier drafts; additionally 14 of 115 task_ids are duplicated. Re-key the source data with synthetic per-row UUIDs and align task_id prefixes with the actual benchmark slugs (`tau_*`, `wa_*`).

suggestions.title: Multi-judge disagreement study on hierarchical annotation
suggestions.description: Run the same 12-row spot-check with two judge models (claude-haiku-4-5 + claude-sonnet-4-6) and compute pairwise verdict agreement plus a confusion matrix. The v1 single-judge accept rate of 33% may be miscalibrated; multi-judge agreement gives a more reliable quality estimate. Estimated cost: ~$0.30 per run.

suggestions.title: Use hierarchical-annotation-v1 to seed Phase 2 scope-conditioning experiments
suggestions.description: The dataset asset is now ready for downstream consumption. Plan a baseline-evaluation task that uses the 102 hierarchy-complete rows to compare scope-conditioned vs scope-unaware agent prompts (B vs G/S/A from the project's research questions).
